evals.name: GPS L1 C/A Waveform (Almanac, Scenario Mode)
evals.description: Generate a GPS L1 C/A baseband waveform using the almanac-based scenario pipeline with real navigation data and physics-based channel impairments
evals.prompt: Generate a 2-second GPS L1 C/A baseband waveform at 5 MHz sample rate. Use a satellite scenario with the GPS SEM almanac for orbit propagation. Receiver location: Boulder, CO (40.015 N, 105.27 W, 1655m altitude). Start time: June 24, 2021, 00:00:48 UTC. Include realistic Doppler, delay, and SNR from orbital dynamics. Encode real navigation data with ephemeris. After generation, print the number of visible satellites, Doppler range, delay range, SNR range, and waveform size.

context.setup: Requires Satellite Communications Toolbox. GPS SDR example helpers must be available.

assertions.description: Uses satelliteScenario for orbit propagation (not hand-rolled Keplerian)
Uses dopplershift() and latency() for physics-based channel parameters
Uses HelperGNSSChannel for channel modeling
Uses HelperGPSNAVDataEncode for real ephemeris nav data (not randi)
SNR in physically realistic range (-20 to 0 dB) because Pt=44.8W and free-space path loss from MEO orbit
Uses Name=Value syntax (not legacy 'Name',Value pairs) and datetime with TimeZone='UTC'
Complete pipeline executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: Galileo E1 Waveform (Almanac, Scenario Mode)
evals.description: Generate a Galileo E1 baseband waveform testing Galileo-specific gotchas: SVID vs PRNID, E1B nav data mapping, HelperAddGalileoSatellitesToScenario, 160W transmit power
evals.prompt: Generate a 1-second Galileo E1 baseband waveform at 25 MHz sample rate. Use a satellite scenario with the Galileo XML almanac for orbit propagation. Receiver location: Munich, Germany (48.1351 N, 11.582 E, 519m altitude). Start time: May 15, 2022, 00:00:00 UTC. Include realistic Doppler, delay, and SNR from orbital dynamics. Encode real Galileo navigation data with ephemeris. After generation, print the number of visible satellites, Doppler range, delay range, SNR range, and waveform size.

context.setup: Requires Satellite Communications Toolbox. Galileo waveform example helpers and galileoAlmanac.xml must be available.

assertions.description: Uses HelperAddGalileoSatellitesToScenario because satellite() does not accept Galileo XML almanac natively
Uses SVID property (not PRNID) because Galileo waveform generator uses SVID
Uses E1B signal type for HelperGalileoNavigationData because E1 waveform maps to E1B nav data
Transmit power is 160 W (Galileo) not 44.8 W (GPS), giving correct SNR
Uses galileoWaveformGenerator system object (not manual modulation or HelperGalileoWaveformGenerator)
Complete pipeline executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: GPS L1 C/A Waveform (Custom Mode)
evals.description: Generate a GPS L1 C/A waveform with user-specified custom channel parameters — no satellite scenario needed
evals.prompt: Generate a 5-second GPS L1 C/A baseband waveform at 5 MHz sample rate with custom channel impairments. Use these values for 4 satellites (PRN 3, 7, 14, 22): Doppler shifts: [2000, -1500, 800, 3200] Hz, propagation delays: [0.072, 0.074, 0.069, 0.078] seconds, SNR: [-10, -12, -11, -9] dB. I'm testing acquisition — random nav bits are fine. Print the waveform size and confirm channel parameters used.

context.setup: Requires Satellite Communications Toolbox. GPS SDR example helpers must be available.

assertions.description: Does NOT create satelliteScenario because custom mode bypasses orbital dynamics entirely
Does NOT call dopplershift() because user provides Doppler values directly
Passes user-specified Doppler values directly to HelperGNSSChannel FrequencyOffset
Uses randi for nav bits because user explicitly said random bits are fine for acquisition testing
Waveform length is 5s * 5MHz = 25,000,000 samples because duration and sample rate are specified
Complete pipeline executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: Galileo E5 Wideband (AltBOC Pitfalls)
evals.description: Generate a Galileo E5 wideband waveform testing critical E5 pitfalls: cell array nav input, I/NAV 5x F/NAV rows, correct center frequency, adequate sample rate
evals.prompt: Generate a 1-second Galileo E5 wideband baseband waveform. Use the Galileo almanac for scenario-based channel. Receiver location: Sydney, Australia (33.8688 S, 151.2093 E, 58m altitude). Include real navigation data with ephemeris. After generation, print the center frequency used, sample rate, number of visible satellites, and confirm the nav data format.

context.setup: Requires Satellite Communications Toolbox. Galileo waveform example helpers and galileoAlmanac.xml must be available.

assertions.description: Center frequency is 1191.795 MHz (E5 midpoint) not 1176.45 (E5a) or 1207.14 (E5b) because E5 wideband uses the midpoint
Nav data passed as cell array {fnavbits, inavbits} because E5 requires both F/NAV and I/NAV components simultaneously
assertions.expected: cell array with two elements for nav data input

assertions.description: Generates F/NAV nav data with SignalType E5a because E5 composite needs separate F/NAV encoding
Sample rate is at least 60 MHz because E5 AltBOC spans ~51 MHz bandwidth
Sets waveform generator SignalType to E5 (not E5a or E5b individually)
Complete pipeline executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: GPS RINEX Path (RINEX-first Preference)
evals.description: Generate a GPS waveform using a RINEX file — verifying RINEX is used for both scenario and nav data, not silently switching to almanac-only path
evals.prompt: I have a GPS RINEX file on the MATLAB path. Generate a 2-second GPS L1 C/A baseband waveform at 5 MHz sample rate using this RINEX file for orbit propagation and navigation data. Receiver location: Goddard Space Flight Center (38.9907 N, 76.8526 W, 53m altitude). Include realistic channel impairments and real navigation data. Tell me which file and epoch you're using.

context.setup: Requires Satellite Communications Toolbox and Navigation Toolbox. GPS E2E example helpers must be available.

assertions.description: Uses rinexread to load the RINEX file because user requested RINEX path
Passes RINEX struct to satellite() for scenario (not almanac filename) because RINEX-first preference
Uses HelperGPSRINEX2Config for nav data because RINEX should drive nav data when RINEX is the scenario source
Agent communicates which file and epoch is being used before generating because agent must be transparent about data source decisions
assertions.expected: Agent states RINEX filename and time range

assertions.description: Start time is set within the RINEX file's time range because mismatched time produces all-NaN latency
Complete pipeline executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: Epoch/Timing Communication and Correctness
evals.description: Test that agent communicates data source epoch, sets start time to match, and uses consistent timing for scenario and nav data
evals.prompt: Generate a GPS L1 C/A waveform with realistic channel impairments from a satellite scenario. Receiver in Tokyo (35.6762 N, 139.6503 E, 40m altitude). Use whatever almanac is available. I need real navigation data for position estimation testing.

context.setup: Requires Satellite Communications Toolbox. GPS SDR example helpers must be available.

assertions.description: Agent communicates which almanac file and its epoch before generating because bundled files are from a fixed past date
assertions.expected: Agent states gpsAlmanac.txt and its epoch date

assertions.description: Start time is set near the almanac epoch (not datetime('now')) because distant start time produces all-NaN latency
Same startTime used for both scenario (sc.StartTime) and nav data encoding (HelperGPSAlmanac2Config) because mismatched times cause silent position errors
assertions.expected: Single startTime variable used in both scenario and nav data calls

assertions.description: Agent communicates sample rate and waveform duration decisions because these affect memory/runtime significantly
assertions.expected: Agent states sample rate and duration before code

assertions.description: Agent communicates number of visible satellites after scenario setup because this validates the epoch/location choice
Complete pipeline executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: NavIC L5 Waveform (RINEX, Scenario Mode)
evals.description: Generate a NavIC L5 waveform testing NavIC-specific gotchas: RINEX-only, no system object, HelperNavICBBWaveform, HelperAddSatellite, GEO/GSO delays, 50W power
evals.prompt: Generate a 2-second NavIC L5 SPS baseband waveform at 24 MHz sample rate. Use a satellite scenario with a NavIC RINEX file available on the MATLAB path. Receiver location: Bangalore, India (12.9716 N, 77.5946 E, 920m altitude). Include realistic Doppler, delay, and SNR from orbital dynamics. Encode real navigation data. After generation, print the number of visible satellites, Doppler range, delay range, SNR range, and waveform size.

context.setup: Requires Satellite Communications Toolbox and Navigation Toolbox. NavIC waveform example and E2E example helpers must be available.

assertions.description: Uses HelperAddSatellite because satellite() does not support NavIC RINEX data directly
Uses HelperNavICBBWaveform because NavIC has no system object in R2026a
Uses HelperNavICRINEX2Config for nav data because NavIC is RINEX-only (no almanac path)
Propagation delay is in GEO/GSO range (119-130 ms) not MEO (67-86 ms) because NavIC uses geostationary/geosynchronous orbits
Transmit power is 50 W (NavIC) not 44.8 (GPS) or 160 (Galileo)
Complete pipeline executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: GPS L5 Waveform (Fewer Visible Satellites)
evals.description: Generate a GPS L5 waveform testing the gotcha that not all GPS satellites broadcast L5 — only Block IIF+ satellites carry it, resulting in fewer visible satellites
evals.prompt: Generate a 2-second GPS L5 baseband waveform at 5 MHz sample rate. Use a satellite scenario with the GPS almanac. Receiver location: Boulder, CO (40.015 N, 105.27 W, 1655m altitude). Start time: June 24, 2021, 00:00:48 UTC. Include realistic channel and real navigation data. Report how many satellites are visible for L5 compared to what you'd expect for L1 C/A.

context.setup: Requires Satellite Communications Toolbox. GPS SDR example helpers must be available.

assertions.description: Uses SignalType l5 and navDataType L5 because GPS L5 has different signal type string than L1 C/A
Center frequency is 1176.45 MHz (L5 band) not 1575.42 MHz (L1) because each GPS signal has a distinct carrier
Agent acknowledges that fewer satellites are visible for L5 (Block IIF+ only) because not all GPS satellites broadcast modernized signals
assertions.expected: Agent mentions L5 availability limited to Block IIF+ or fewer visible satellites

assertions.description: Filters PRN list against nav data availability because some scenario-visible satellites may not have L5 nav data in the almanac
assertions.expected: Intersection or filtering of visible PRNs against available nav data

assertions.description: Visible L5 satellites are 4-6 (not 8-12 like L1 C/A) because only a subset broadcasts L5
Complete pipeline executes without error via mcp__matlab__evaluate_matlab_code

evals.name: NavIC L1 SPS Waveform (SBOC/IZ4 Modulation)
evals.description: Generate a NavIC L1 SPS waveform testing L1-specific pitfalls: SBOC modulation, IZ4 codes from .mat file (not gnssCACode), LDPC parity check matrix, different modulation path from L5/S
evals.prompt: Generate a 0.2-second NavIC L1 SPS baseband waveform at 24 MHz sample rate. Use a satellite scenario with a NavIC RINEX file available on the MATLAB path. Receiver location: Bangalore, India (12.9716 N, 77.5946 E, 920m altitude). Include realistic channel impairments and real navigation data. After generation, confirm which modulation and spreading code approach is used for L1 (it differs from L5/S).

context.setup: Requires Satellite Communications Toolbox and Navigation Toolbox. NavIC L1 example helpers and NavICL1SpreadingCodeInitialConditions.mat must be available.

assertions.description: Uses NavIC L1-SPS signal type for SBOC modulation path (different from L5/S standard BOC)
Loads IZ4 spreading codes from .mat file because gnssCACode does not support NavIC L1-SPS
Does NOT call gnssCACode() for L1 spreading code generation because L1 uses IZ4 codes from .mat file
Center frequency is 1575.42 MHz (L1 band) not 1176.45 MHz (L5)
References L1Dataset support files (LDPC matrix or spreading codes from downloaded dataset)
References or loads L1 support data files (either downloads via websave or loads pre-existing .mat files)
Uses L1-specific HelperNavICBBWaveform signature with L1PRNInit struct as 3rd argument (different from L5/S where 3rd arg is empty [])
assertions.expected: HelperNavICBBWaveform call passes L1PRNInit struct and single PRNID
